component.name: Helix
component.summary: Privacy-first music player — stream, visualize, discover

description.p: Helix is a privacy-first desktop music player built with Rust, Tauri, and Svelte. It lets you search, stream, organize, and visualize music from multiple sources without requiring an account.

Unlike browser-based players, Helix runs the audio pipeline natively: stream resolution via yt-dlp, decoding with Symphonia, playback with cpal, and FFT-driven real-time visualizations. This gives lower latency, broader format support, and real audio data for spectrum and oscilloscope views.

ul.li: Search and stream from YouTube, SoundCloud, and Bandcamp
Real-time audio visualizations including spectrum and oscilloscope modes
Queue management, playlists, artist favorites, and listening history
No tracking, no cookies, and no account requirement
Automatic yt-dlp management on first use
Initial Linux release packaging for Helix
YouTube and SoundCloud search and playback
Playlists, artist favorites, and recent items on the home screen
Automatic yt-dlp download on first remote use
Native audio playback and visualization pipeline

screenshot.caption: Search and stream music from multiple sources
Playback controls and queue in the desktop player
Discover recent tracks and recent lists from the home screen
